name: Good Neighbor Hummus
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Garbanzo beans (2 16 oz. cans, rinsed and drained)
1/4 cup Tamari or soy sauce (preferably tamari)
recipeInstructions:
1. Put beans, tahini, lemon juice, 1/4 cup olive oil, tamari, garlic, and peppers in a food processor.
2. Blend until smooth.
3. Transfer to serving bowl.
4. Drizzle with olive oil; sprinkle with paprika and parsley if desired.
Note: In her column notes, Colombo mentions that chili paste can be purchased at Oriental food markets. This recipe makes no mention of chili paste, although it was an ingredient in the Good Neighbor Szechuan noodles. Given its slight bite when purchased at Good Neighbors, you may want to experiment with adding a little chili paste for extra flavor.
Absolutely great with unsalted blue corn chips! Have eaten many times but haven't made at home yet. It's delicious and sells for $4.50/lb. at Good Neighbor Foods Co-op Cafe.
